Pause for effect

Hook
Punchy
Never before have we grown this fast.
Not only did we open a clinic, but we also broke into a new market.
Only after the pilot succeeded did investors take us seriously.
Rarely do audiences remember the data; they remember the story.
Eliane, your investor update is full of great news. But can I be honest? Right now, it does not land.
That is exactly why I called you. Never before have I had numbers this good, and yet the room stayed flat.
Because you understate everything. You need a hook, and you need gravitas.
So how do I open? With the big number?
Open with a soundbite. Not only should you state the milestone, but you should also pause for effect.
And when I reach the growth figure?
Drive it home with conviction. Only when you slow down does a number truly hit home.
Then I end with what I want from them.
Exactly. Rarely does an update end with a clear call to action. Do that, and you will command the room.
Rarely does an update end with a clear call to action.
Slow down, pause for effect, and let it hit home.

Eliane, it is Julian. I listened to the draft of your keynote, and I want to push you to make it land. Right now the content is strong, but the delivery understates it. Here is the thing: never before have you had a story this good, so do not bury it. Open with a hook. Not only should you state the milestone, but you should also pause for effect and let it hit home. When you get to the big number, drive it home with conviction. Rarely does an audience remember your data. They remember the one soundbite that carried gravitas. And end with a clear call to action. Only when you speak as if every word matters will the room truly listen. You have earned this, Eliane. Now go and command the room.

Good evening. I want to begin with one number. Two years ago, this clinic did not exist. Never before had our city had a center like this one, and not only did we open it, but we did it against the odds. Let me be clear about what that means. Only after countless setbacks did the first breakthrough come. No sooner had we found our footing than the demand doubled. I will not understate it. This has been the hardest and the most important work of my life. So here is my call to action. Rarely do we get to build something that outlasts us. This is that chance. Join us, and let us make the next chapter land even harder than the first.
